basics.name: Amandeep Singh "Aman" Talwar
basics.label: AI-Enabled Operations Strategist · Developer Experience · Large-Scale Program Leadership
basics.summary: Strategy and program leader with 20+ years driving large-scale, complex initiatives across software and semiconductor organizations, and 7+ years managing globally distributed teams. Operate at the intersection of business, product, and engineering — structuring highly complex information to align senior stakeholders and convert ambiguous goals into repeatable, deployable solutions. Currently leading the adoption of AI and agentic tooling (MCP) to re-imagine and automate content and developer workflows: defining the strategy, building the toolchain, and operationalizing it into release processes.

work.name: Autodesk
highlights: Define strategy and standards for AI-assisted, developer-facing solutions and own release readiness across cross-functional workflows.
Lead the development and deployment of AI/agentic tooling (MCP) that automates standards-based evaluation and solution generation — scaling output without scaling headcount.
Drive large, complex initiatives spanning open API specification, Jira migration, and platform integration, influencing priorities across product, engineering, and design.
Build repeatable solution content — best practices, standards, reference material — and embed it into engineering release processes; raise the talent bar through senior-level hiring.

work.name: Microchip
highlights: Managed a global team and contributed to business-unit strategic planning, aligning quality, timing, and process to company objectives.
Owned capacity planning, risk forecasting, and stakeholder-approved program plans; led an enterprise-wide FrameMaker → DITA XML transformation with full change management.
Identified recurring solution issues across teams and collaborated cross-functionally to mitigate future risk.

work.name: Xilinx
work.position: Documentation Manager / Senior Staff Technical Writer
highlights: Led a milestone Converged Access release across 3 teams and 3 business units, requiring extensive stakeholder alignment across conflicting priorities.
Acted as PO/PM for XML transformation initiatives; set long-term solution strategy and drove org-wide adoption.
Drove automation of 600+ CLI references and API generation (Doxygen/Sphinx/XSLT), a large-scale efficiency and consistency gain.
Selected for the Management Development Solution leadership program.

work.name: Freescale Semiconductors (now NXP)
highlights: Led the global Doxygen strategy, defining standards across worldwide business units and building automation that eliminated days of manual effort per release.
Earned Best Technical Poster at the IDC Technical Symposium, multiple peer and appreciation awards, and Community Volunteer of the Year 2008.

work.name: Intoto Software (now NXP)
highlights: Built the BHARATI solution portal in JSP/MySQL.

education.studyType: PG Honors Diploma in Computer Applications
